brief_justification: Paper A derives epistemic/aleatoric uncertainty measures by decomposing a subjective risk built from a strictly proper loss (an information-theoretic/learning-theory estimation principle), whereas paper B is a survey of social-simulation paradigms (agent-based models, LLM-driven agents, digital twins) with no governing equation or estimation principle; there is no transferable machinery and no coherent role-for-role mapping between a loss-decomposition and a taxonomy of simulation methods.
